title: aglaia indica
intro: Aglaia indica, also known as the Malabar Thorn or Ceylon Ironwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae, native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as aglaiin, aglacones, and other flavonoids. The health benefits of Aglaia indica include its potential in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Aglaia indica is often prepared as a herbal decoction, infusion, or powder, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
